meta.title: Mercedes SL55 SL63 AMG Engine Replacement Cost UK — Compare Quotes
meta.description: Every Mercedes‑Benz SL55 / SL63 AMG engine — M113K and M157. R230 and R231 generations, real UK costs and reliability. Get free quotes from 25+ vetted Mercedes specialists today.

@graph.name: Mercedes‑Benz SL55 / SL63 AMG Engine Era Map
Mercedes‑Benz SL55 / SL63 AMG Engine Codes

mainEntity.name: How much does a Mercedes‑Benz SL55 / SL63 AMG engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned M113K engine typically costs £4,000–£8,000 supply only; the M157 costs £6,000–£16,000. Fitting adds £1,000–£1,800. Main dealer new engines can exceed £30,000. M157 replacement motors average £14,167. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your car.

mainEntity.name: Is the Mercedes‑Benz SL55 / SL63 AMG engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: It depends on the engine. The M113K (SL55) is rated ★★★★☆ — widely considered bulletproof with 'galactic miles' reported by owners. The M157 (SL63) is rated ★★★☆☆ — early examples (2010–2014) have timing chain, oil leak, and cylinder scoring risks. Post‑2014 M157s are more reliable.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Mercedes‑Benz SL55 / SL63 AMG?

acceptedAnswer.text: M113K issues: supercharger coolant pump failure, valve cover oil leaks, AMG‑specific idler pulley wear, and the plastic selector peg in the five‑speed gearbox. M157 issues: timing chain wear (early cars), camshaft position sensor oil leaks, valve cover gasket leaks, cylinder scoring, and coolant system leaks.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Mercedes‑Benz SL55 / SL63 AMG engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on vehicle value. Against a well‑kept SL55 AMG worth £15,000–£25,000, a £4,000–£8,000 recon is a manageable 16–53% of the vehicle's value — worth it for a well‑maintained example. For a SL63 AMG worth £20,000–£40,000, a £6,000–£16,000 recon is more significant but often worthwhile given the SL's status and strong retained values.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Mercedes‑Benz SL55 / SL63 AMG engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. The SL55 AMG uses the M113K (M113.991) 5.4‑litre supercharged V8. The SL63 AMG uses the M157 (157.983) 5.5‑litre twin‑turbo V8. Tell us your registration date and we'll confirm it for free.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the M157's timing chain, or does it need a new engine?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — if caught early. A timing chain, tensioner and guide replacement (£1,500–£4,000) is far cheaper than a catastrophic chain failure (£6,000–£16,000 engine replacement). Install the updated chain tensioner and non-return valves. At the first sign of cold‑start rattle, book an inspection immediately. Early M157s (2010–2012) are most affected.

eraMap.title: The SL55 / SL63 AMG THROUGH TIME — Era Map

rows.eraNote: The supercharged M113K — 493 bhp, bulletproof when maintained; valve cover seals, oil leaks and supercharger coolant pump are the main watchpoints.
The twin‑turbo M157 — 536–577 bhp; timing chain wear, oil leaks and cylinder scoring are the defining weaknesses.

replacementCosts.h2: Mercedes-Benz SL55 / SL63 AMG Engine Replacement Costs

rows.bestFor: Insurance jobs, warranty claims; M157 replacement motors average £14,167+

replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting an SL AMG engine typically takes 14–18 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer). AMG-specific parts are significantly more expensive than standard Mercedes components — an idler pulley for the AMG is £350 vs £50 for the standard version.

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: Mercedes-Benz SL55 / SL63 AMG — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Straightforward pump replacement; catch it before performance loss
Replace seals; catch it before low oil levels
Replace with AMG-specific part; don't let it fail
If caught early; update tensioner and non-return valves

rows.problem: M157 cam position sensor oil leaks
rows.whenItMakesSense: Preventative — catch it before oil reaches the ECU
Bore scoring is serious; sleeve and rebuild required
Replace with aluminium peg; dealer may replace entire unit

buyingChecks.title: Buying This SL55 / SL63 AMG? Check These First
buyIf: * Full Mercedes service history with documented oil changes
* SL63 M157 post‑2014 (timing chain issues resolved)
* SL63 M157 with documented timing chain tensioner update
* Evidence of charge cooler pump replacement (M113K) or cam sensor seals (M157)
avoidIf: * M157 with cold‑start rattle and no timing chain history — the single most important check on the SL63
* Oil in camshaft sensor connectors — potential ECU damage risk
* Any evidence of cylinder scoring (misfires, loss of compression)
* Poor service history — AMG engines are unforgiving of neglect

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The SL55 AMG's M113K is widely considered one of Mercedes' most bulletproof performance engines — a supercharged V8 masterpiece with "galactic miles" reported by owners. The SL63 AMG's M157 is a more complex twin-turbo engine with known timing chain, oil leak, and cylinder scoring risks — particularly on early examples. The M113K is the more reliable choice; the M157 offers more power but demands diligent maintenance. Buy on documented history. If you're replacing, a reconditioned unit with a 12‑month warranty is the sweet spot.

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Mercedes‑Benz SL55 / SL63 AMG (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Model & Engine — Confirm which engine the car has (M113K for SL55 AMG R230, M157 for SL63 AMG R231)
* Cold Start — Listen for rattling noise on startup (M157 timing chain warning — the most critical check)
* Service History — Including which engine era (M113K for R230 2003–2011, M157 for R231 2012–2020) — see the Era Map above
* Oil Leaks — Check for oil spots under the car or burning oil smell (M113K valve covers, M157 cam sensors)
* Cam Sensor Connectors — Disconnect and check for oil — oil in the plug could mean a new ECU (M157)
* Supercharger Health — Any loss of power? M113K charge cooler pump failure risk
* Idler Pulley — Any squeaking or chirping? AMG-specific part is expensive
* Transmission — Any hesitation from Park to Drive? R230 plastic selector peg issue
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Any unresolved timing, boost, or emissions issues?

items.label: Most Common Failure
